content: How does the author identify himself at the opening of 3 John?
explanation: 3 John 1:1 — 'The elder to the beloved Gaius, whom I love in truth.' As in 2 John, the author calls himself 'the elder' — humble yet carrying spiritual authority.

content: To whom is 3 John addressed?
explanation: 3 John 1:1 — '...to the beloved Gaius, whom I love in truth.' Gaius is a believer commended for his hospitality to traveling preachers.

content: In 3 John 1:2, what does the elder pray for Gaius?
explanation: 3 John 1:2 — 'Beloved, I pray that all may go well with you and that you may be in good health, as it goes well with your soul.'
options: To receive a gift of church leadership
That all may go well and he be in good health, as his soul prospers

content: According to 3 John 1:3, what made the elder greatly rejoice?
explanation: 3 John 1:3 — 'For I rejoiced greatly when the brothers came and testified to your truth, as indeed you are walking in the truth.'
options: That Gaius had held a great feast
That the brothers testified Gaius was walking in the truth

content: According to 3 John 1:4, what is the elder's greatest joy?
explanation: 3 John 1:4 — 'I have no greater joy than to hear that my children are walking in the truth.'
options: To hear that his children walk in the truth
To see the church growing in numbers
To be honored by the authorities

content: According to 3 John 1:5, what faithful thing does Gaius do?
explanation: 3 John 1:5 — 'Beloved, it is a faithful thing you do in all your efforts for these brothers, strangers as they are.' He welcomes both familiar believers and traveling preachers alike.
options: Caring for the brothers, even those who are strangers

content: According to 3 John 1:6, how does the elder urge Gaius to send the brothers on their way?
explanation: 3 John 1:6 — '...You will do well to send them on their journey in a manner worthy of God.' 'Worthy of God' means providing provisions, prayer, and needed support.
options: In a manner worthy of God

content: According to 3 John 1:7, for what have the brothers gone out?
explanation: 3 John 1:7 — 'For they have gone out for the sake of the name, accepting nothing from the Gentiles.'
options: Seeking a living in foreign lands
For the sake of the Name

content: According to 3 John 1:7, from whom do the traveling preachers accept nothing?
explanation: 3 John 1:7 — '...accepting nothing from the Gentiles.' They do not rely on unbelievers' resources, avoiding any confusion about their motives and honoring the principle of being supported by the saints.

content: According to 3 John 1:8, why should believers support such preachers?
explanation: 3 John 1:8 — 'Therefore we ought to support people like these, that we may be fellow workers for the truth.' Supporting missionaries is how believers share in the work of the gospel.
options: To be praised in the church
That we may be fellow workers for the truth

content: Who in the church 'likes to put himself first' according to 3 John 1:9?
explanation: 3 John 1:9 — 'I have written something to the church, but Diotrephes, who likes to put himself first, does not acknowledge our authority.' This desire to be first (philoprōteuōn) is the root of Diotrephes's many offenses.

content: According to 3 John 1:9, whom does Diotrephes refuse to welcome?
explanation: 3 John 1:9 — '...Diotrephes, who likes to put himself first, does not acknowledge our authority.' He rejects the elder himself — the one holding spiritual authority in the region.
options: The elder and those with him

content: According to 3 John 1:10, which wicked deeds of Diotrephes will the elder bring up when he comes?
explanation: 3 John 1:10 — '...talking wicked nonsense against us. And not content with that, he refuses to welcome the brothers, and also stops those who want to and puts them out of the church.'
options: Talking wicked nonsense, refusing brothers, putting out those who welcome them
Teaching false doctrine about the resurrection

content: According to 3 John 1:11, whoever does good is from whom?
explanation: 3 John 1:11 — 'Beloved, do not imitate evil but imitate good. Whoever does good is from God; whoever does evil has not seen God.' Action reveals spiritual nature.

content: According to 3 John 1:11, whoever does evil has not what?
explanation: 3 John 1:11 — '...whoever does evil has not seen God.' 'Seen' here means to know God intimately — evil deeds prove the absence of true fellowship with Him.
options: Will be expelled from the church

content: According to 3 John 1:12, who received a good testimony from everyone?
explanation: 3 John 1:12 — 'Demetrius has received a good testimony from everyone, and from the truth itself. We also add our testimony, and you know that our testimony is true.' Demetrius was likely the bearer of this letter.

content: At the close of 3 John, what does the elder express hope for?
explanation: 3 John 1:13-14 — 'I had much to write to you, but I would rather not write with pen and ink. I hope to see you soon, and we will talk face to face.'
options: To see Gaius soon and talk face to face
To go on mission to Asia
To gather all the apostles in one place

content: According to 3 John 1:10, which offenses does Diotrephes commit? (select all that apply)
explanation: 3 John 1:10 — '...talking wicked nonsense against us... he refuses to welcome the brothers, and also stops those who want to and puts them out of the church.' Three clear offenses; the verse makes no mention of embezzlement.
options: Speaking wicked nonsense against the elder
Stopping and putting out of the church those who welcome them

content: According to 3 John 1:2, what things does the elder pray for concerning Gaius? (select all that apply)
explanation: 3 John 1:2 — 'I pray that all may go well with you and that you may be in good health, as it goes well with your soul.' Three items: all going well, good health, prospering soul. No mention of great wealth.
options: To have great and abundant possessions
That all may go well with him
That he be in good bodily health

content: According to 3 John 1:12, from which sources has Demetrius received a good testimony? (select all that apply)
explanation: 3 John 1:12 — 'Demetrius has received a good testimony from everyone, and from the truth itself. We also add our testimony...' Three sources: everyone, the truth itself, and the elder together with those accompanying him.
options: From the elder and those with him
